name: Bermuda (United Kingdom)

colors.symbolism: the field of the British Red Ensign
the saltire and cross of the Union Flag canton; sails of the wrecked ship in the coat of arms
the field of the Union Flag canton; the sea around the wrecked ship

description: A British Red Ensign — red field with the Union Flag in the upper-hoist canton — defaced in the fly with the coat of arms of Bermuda: a red lion holding a shield showing the wreck of the Sea Venture striking the rocks.
history: Adopted on 4 October 1910 as the civil ensign of Bermuda. Bermuda is the only British Overseas Territory with a current civilian flag based on the Red Ensign rather than the Blue Ensign; the Blue Ensign with the Bermuda badge is reserved as a government ensign. The coat of arms was granted in 1910 and references the 1609 wreck of the Sea Venture, which led to the English settlement of the islands.

features.symbolism: the field of the British Red Ensign — Bermuda is unusual among British Overseas Territories in using a defaced Red Ensign rather than a Blue Ensign as its civil flag
Bermuda's status as a British Overseas Territory
a red lion holding a scrolled escutcheon depicting the wreck of the Sea Venture (1609) on the reefs of Bermuda — the event that led to the islands' settlement by the English
